body_txt: Wanting Is Better Than Having is one of the Stock Aesops , which teaches that one's desire and anticipation for something is often better than the actual result. After all, desires and expectations are infinite and boundless, whereas the reality is confined by various flaws and limitations — which were overlooked during the expectant daydreaming. Usually appears at the end of a story after a character has been wildly pursuing something. When they eventually gets the object of their desire, it seems it fails to live up to the unrealistic demands they've built up in their mind. As An Aesop , it serves as a reminder that part of life is the struggle, and that's what keeps you going. If you had everything you wanted, you would have nothing to shoot for. And besides - getting what you want isn't always that good for you. A Wish Fulfillment trope and a milder, non-magical form of Be Careful What You Wish For . Also see All That Glitters , and Loving a Shadow . May overlap with Lonely at the Top , Popularity Cycle , Vengeance Feels Empty , And Then What? , Victory Is Boring , But Not Too Challenging , Broken Pedestal , and Grass Is Greener . A work-specific subtrope of this is Disappointing Promotion . For a similar Aesop, see It's the Journey That Counts , which shares many a lesson with this trope. Compare with Forbidden Fruit , where the wanting part comes from not being allowed to have. Overlaps with Guys Want to Chase if a guy is more interested in chasing a girl than actually being with her.
